hard.next_step: Confidence 50% 18th May - Met the HOP (Zyad) & VP (Jason) - current thinking is that all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues (all of their conferences & exhibitions for near future are cancelled/postponed) - have planted some seeds around my blog post on investing in procuretech to protect their margins and strengthen economics now and to prepare for the reopening and rescheduling of the backlog), seemed to resonate with VP who will discuss with upper mgt, (I offered to present to them and/or bring Carl in in helpful). 11th May- Regional unrest slowing down projects at present- KIV and keep pushing to accelerate. 17th April - Response from Proj Lead: "T hanks for checking in Dan. Things are slowly but surely improving however the event/exhibition confidence and surge like previous months will take some time before our calendar is reset with firm dates/expected foot fall. Budgetary controls are still in place so shall go with the flow for now till we fully rebound". Will move back to Q2 and monitor. 19th Mar - The war has forced them to reassess priorities, the project is paused for now, but (at this stage) still expecting to restart in Q1 (their Q2) 3rd Mar - Nudged Zyad for an update, session to evaluate options is now mid-March, will synch with him asap afterwards. 23rd Feb - Submitted revised scope budgetary estimate pricing ahead of management meeting end Feb. 12th Feb - Spoke to Zyad, unfortunately scope has reduced back to CLM + iSupplier repository only at this phase (though interested in other modules later) - likely to be too small in scope now or min pricing will make us uncompetitive, will get the pricing from Raj & discuss with Carl - reducing confidence to 5%, 10th Oct - Budgetary Estimate provided - next steps formal RFP once budget confirmed 7th Oct - Requested Budgetary Estimate, also AVP will be visiting us in DPW Amsterdam 11th Sept - Zyad attended the Dubai World Tour (AVP also invited), positive conversation, but will be a formal process. Aug 26th - Courtesy reach-out to Sami to see if we can be of any assistance in the process. July 30th - Intro call with Samy - focus is on CLM, just doing initial research prior to gaining management approval for a formal RFP.
hard.ais_why:
• Across 1,709 chars of NextStep covering eight dated entries from Jul 2025 to May 2026 ‚Äî including disco call, budgetary estimate, scope reduction and war-related delays ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic, or GenAI. The 12 Feb 2026 entry: 'Spoke to Zyad, unfortunately scope has reduced back to CLM + iSupplier repository only at this phase (though interested in other modules later) - likely to be too small in scope now or min pricing will make us uncompetitive, will get the pricing from Raj.' Scope is shrinking not expanding into AI.
• On 17 Apr 2026 Project Lead Zyad responded: 'event/exhibition confidence and surge like previous months will take some time before our calendar is reset with firm dates/expected foot fall. Budgetary controls are still in place so shall go with the flow for now.' On 11 May 2026: 'Regional unrest slowing down projects at present.' The deal is paused with shrunk scope; Merlin is bundled in Products but never named in any rep-buyer dialogue.
• Confidence is Medium. Scope contraction to 'CLM + iSupplier repository only' eliminates whatever AI positioning might have existed, and Zyad's communications are all budget/timing-focused rather than capability-driven. Held at AI Resistant given absence of AI keyword and shrinking deal footprint.

north_star_verdict.headline: Force-majeure pause, not a competitive loss: regional conflict/blockade has frozen all non-essential DWTC spend and cancelled their event calendar, so the CLM decision is parked with scope shrunk to CLM plus an iSupplier repository. The buyer stays constructively engaged (last inbound Zyad Khan 18 May 2026) and signals intent to restart 'later in the year', but no restart date is set and the deal just changed hands from Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim on 29 Jun 2026.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 8 Aug 2025 (~332 days old); in Formal Evaluation the entire time with no stage-submission dates populated. Close date 31 Dec 2026 is 178 days out, pushed there from 30 Jul 2026 on 8 Jun 2026 (the latest of six close-date slips since Mar 2026). Paused since 19 Mar 2026 (~109 days). Last buyer inbound 18 May 2026 (49 days ago); last rep touch 25 May 2026. A light 2-module scope means 4-5 months from restart to close is feasible, so 31 Dec 2026 is credible only if budget reopens by early Q4 2026.
evidence: Deal paused 19 Mar 2026: Next_Step__c 'The war has forced them to reassess priorities, the project is paused for now, but (at this stage) still expecting to restart in Q1 (their Q2).'

18 May 2026: owner met Zyad Khan (HOP) and Jason Tranter (VP) - 'all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues (all of their conferences & exhibitions for near future are cancelled/postponed)'; seeds planted around a margin-protection investment case 'seemed to resonate with VP who will discuss with upper mgt'.
17 Apr 2026: Zyad Khan - 'Budgetary controls are still in place so shall go with the flow for now till we fully rebound.'
12 Feb 2026: scope reduced back to CLM + iSupplier repository only, 'likely to be too small in scope now or min pricing will make us uncompetitive'; confidence cut to 5%.
Buyer not disengaged: constructive inbound 18 May 2026 arranging an in-person office meeting; tone positive throughout the email trail.
Owner reassigned Dan Quinn -> Mohamad Alhakim (RVP) on 29 Jun 2026, so the new owner has not yet re-established the relationship.

metrics.narrative: The value case is real but still qualitative and audit-driven, not quantified. On the 11 Feb 2026 call Zyad Khan said audit 'have been mocking this contract management or dashboard as something obsolete' and that he wants 'a 3 year TCO model around it, not just for the ROI sake because I'm also building a case with audit'. He named a concrete self-funding cost lever: phasing out '5,000 envelopes from Docusign' to redirect that budget toward the eSignature/Certinal module. No hard savings, cycle-time, headcount, or error-rate numbers have been stated, so the quantified 3-year TCO Zyad asked for is the outstanding deliverable that would convert directional pain into a fundable business case.
sources: SF Next_Step_History__c 23 Feb 2026 (revised budgetary pricing submitted)

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits above the mapped contacts. On 11 Feb 2026 Zyad Khan said 'I report into the senior vice president of IT' and that this unbudgeted spend 'has to go through an unbudgeted approval' cleared by a quarterly management committee. That SVP of IT is the true economic buyer, named only by role and never engaged directly. Jason Tranter (AVP Procurement & Contracts, Salesforce Decision Maker) is the most senior mapped buyer and on 18 May 2026 committed to 'discuss with upper mgt' the margin-protection case, so sponsorship today runs through Jason and the committee rather than the CFO. Naming and reaching the SVP of IT is the open EB move once budget reopens.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are functional and cost-driven. The buyer wants off-the-shelf CLM authoring plus a contract repository with renewal/expiry alerting (Zyad Khan on the 30 Sep 2025 demo asked specifically about 'renewal management of the repository' auto-populating expiry dates), ERP compatibility with their Oracle EBS (Samyukta Rajendran confirmed 'We're currently using Oracle EBS' on 13 Feb 2026), scanned-document ingestion (Samyukta on 30 Sep 2025), and a price low enough to clear an unbudgeted approval. Zyad explicitly weighed and rejected an in-house/audit build in favour of a standard off-the-shelf product on 11 Feb 2026. The decisive criterion now is affordability under the budget freeze, not features.
sources: Avoma call 30 Sep 2025 (Zyad Khan, Samyukta Rajendran)

decision_process.narrative: The process read from the call sequence is a procurement-led, IT-sponsored evaluation gated by budget. The 30 Sep 2025 demo assembled the full committee - Jason Tranter (AVP Procurement), Zyad Khan and Yamen Hmidan (Associate Directors, Procurement / Digital Transformation), Farzan Almarzooqi (IT Applications) and Samyukta Rajendran (Data Analyst) - with Zyad as day-to-day point of contact and Jason saying 'I'll get some internal feedback as well from our IT team about how they see this integrating.' The stated path is budgetary estimate -> quarterly management-committee approval for unbudgeted spend -> formal RFP once budget is confirmed (Next_Step__c 10 Oct 2025). That path is frozen by the conflict; no RFP has opened.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 10 Oct 2025 ('formal RFP once budget confirmed')

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics (MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal/InfoSec review, procurement channel) have not been reached because the deal stalled before an RFP and never left budgetary pricing. This is a light two-module CLM footprint, so the paper trail will be an Order Form plus SOW rather than a full-suite MSA negotiation. The only live forcing functions today are commercial, not legal: an unbudgeted-spend approval through the quarterly management committee, and the DocuSign envelope renewal Zyad wants to redirect as a budget source. Visibility will come only once budget reopens and a formal scope/RFP is confirmed; asking Zyad for the intended procurement and approval steps at restart would create it.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is specific and buyer-articulated. Internal audit is 'mocking this contract management or dashboard as something obsolete' and recommended an in-house build, which Zyad Khan rejected on 11 Feb 2026 to avoid diverting the IT development team from revenue-generating work. DWTC manages 'a very diverse' multi-event supply chain ('3 different contents, 3 different supply chains') with no modern CLM, wants automated renewal/expiry management, and is paying for ~5,000 DocuSign envelopes it wants to consolidate. Pain owner is Zyad Khan (Procurement & Contracts), with audit as the internal pressure source. The overriding blocker is external and financial: the conflict has frozen all non-essential spend.

champion.narrative: Zyad Khan (Associate Director - Procurement, Contracts and ESG; Salesforce Evaluator, functioning as champion) is a developing champion. His advocacy is concrete: he ruled out the audit-suggested in-house build in Zycus's favour, framed a modular 'small piecemeal things and then gradually scale it up' adoption, planned a 3-year TCO model, and has a line to economic authority (he reports to the SVP of IT and appeared jointly with Jason Tranter on 18 May 2026). What is still missing to make him 'strong': he has not yet presented to the management committee or secured upper-management escalation, and the promised TCO/business case is unfinished. Engagement is active but paused by the spend freeze (last contact 18 May 2026), not cooling.

competition.narrative: This is effectively a sole-source pursuit with no active peer-vendor bake-off. The audit-recommended in-house build was explicitly declined by Zyad Khan on 11 Feb 2026 ('building in house would compromise some of the revenue work we do... I'll bring an off the shelf product'). The Salesforce-listed peers (iCertis, Sirion Labs, Ironclad, Agiloft) have never been named by the buyer on any call or in Next_Step__c and read as dormant CRM placeholders. The real current threat is do-nothing / status quo: the conflict-driven budget freeze has indefinitely delayed the decision, and Zyad's own worry is that 'min pricing will make us uncompetitive'. We win by keeping the CLM framed as audit-risk mitigation and margin protection during the downturn, plus DocuSign consolidation as self-funding ROI.

critical_signals.text: Sole-source: DWTC is evaluating only Zycus, rejected an audit-suggested in-house build on 11 Feb 2026, and the real threat is a conflict-driven budget freeze that has paused all non-essential spend.

At the 18 May 2026 in-person meeting Zyad Khan and Jason Tranter confirmed spend is paused until the region rebounds, but a margin-protection investment pitch 'seemed to resonate' with Jason, who will raise it with upper management.

Scope has shrunk to CLM plus an iSupplier repository and Zyad fears the resulting minimum price 'will make us uncompetitive', so affordability under an unbudgeted approval is the deciding commercial risk, not features.
The account changed hands from Dan Quinn to RVP Mohamad Alhakim on 29 Jun 2026, so the primary buyer relationship (Zyad Khan) now needs to be re-established by the new owner.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum is frozen by force majeure, not lost. After a full-committee demo (30 Sep 2025) and budgetary pricing rounds (Oct 2025, revised 23 Feb 2026), the regional conflict paused the project on 19 Mar 2026; scope was cut to CLM + iSupplier repository (12 Feb 2026) and the close date slipped six times to 31 Dec 2026. The buyer stayed constructively engaged through 18 May 2026, then ownership moved to Mohamad Alhakim on 29 Jun 2026.

items.change: Ownership reassigned Dan Quinn -> Mohamad Alhakim (RVP) and amount set to $110k
In-person meeting with Zyad Khan and Jason Tranter; spend confirmed paused, margin-protection case to go to upper management
Project paused due to regional conflict; priorities reassessed
Revised-scope budgetary pricing submitted ahead of management meeting
Scope reduced to CLM + iSupplier repository only; confidence cut to 5%

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the sole vendor under active consideration; there is no live peer-vendor bake-off. The strongest CURRENT threat is do-nothing / status quo, driven by the conflict-induced budget freeze that has paused all non-essential DWTC spend since 19 Mar 2026, compounded by Zyad's fear that a minimum-scope price will be uncompetitive. The audit-recommended in-house build was declined by Zyad Khan on 11 Feb 2026 in Zycus's favour. The Salesforce-listed peers (iCertis, Sirion Labs, Ironclad, Agiloft) have never been named by the buyer and remain dormant CRM placeholders; no re-ranking this run as no new buyer call surfaced any of them.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / status quo
competitors.quote: all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues
competitors.how_we_win: Frame CLM as audit-risk mitigation and downturn margin protection; make DocuSign envelope consolidation the self-funding ROI so the spend clears an unbudgeted approval.

competitors.name: In-house build
competitors.quote: building in house would compromise some of the revenue work we do with the development team here. So I would rather not make them do that, and I'll bring an off the shelf product.
competitors.how_we_win: Off-the-shelf speed-to-value versus diverting the IT team; modular phased deployment minimizes upfront commitment.

competitors.name: iCertis
competitors.source: SF Competitors__c (no buyer mention on any call)
competitors.how_we_win: No active evaluation signal; monitor for late-stage emergence if the pause extends into 2027.

competitors.source: SF Competitors__c (no buyer mention on any call)
competitors.how_we_win: No active evaluation signal; monitor for late-stage emergence if the pause extends into 2027.

competitors.how_we_win: No active evaluation signal; monitor for late-stage emergence if the pause extends into 2027.

competitors.source: SF Competitors__c (no buyer mention on any call)
competitors.how_we_win: No active evaluation signal; monitor for late-stage emergence if the pause extends into 2027.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Functional fit is strong for a light CLM footprint; the exposure is entirely price-under-budget-freeze, not capability.

items.criterion: Off-the-shelf CLM authoring + repository with renewal/expiry alerting
items.quote: the renewal management of the repository that probably would allow us to manage the timelines of renewals as well as flagging some of those budgets

items.criterion: Price low enough to clear an unbudgeted approval at minimum scope
items.quote: likely to be too small in scope now or min pricing will make us uncompetitive

items.deliverable: Confirm the outcome of the upper-management budget review and a restart/RFP date

items.quote: shall go with the flow for now till we fully rebound
I report into the senior vice president of IT
I would want to present a 3 year TCO model around it... I'm also building a case with audit

best_practice_check.summary: This is a paused, sole-source, price-sensitive deal that just changed owner; the win levers are re-establishing the relationship, funding the case, and staying warm until budget reopens.
flags: New owner Mohamad Alhakim must re-introduce himself to Zyad Khan and Jason Tranter before the next budget cycle so the relationship survives the Dan Quinn handover.
Convert Zyad's audit pressure into a quantified 3-year TCO that funds itself via DocuSign envelope consolidation, so the spend clears an unbudgeted approval when the freeze lifts.

Reach past Zyad to the SVP of IT (the real budget owner) through Jason Tranter's committed upper-management conversation, since a minimum-scope price needs an executive sponsor to survive the committee.

items.name: Jason Tranter
items.title: AVP - Procurement & Contracts EMBA, BSc (Hons), MCIArb, MRICS
items.risk: Passive sponsorship: committed 18 May 2026 to raise the margin-protection case with upper management but no confirmed exec follow-up; event-recovery priorities may crowd out CLM when budget reopens.

items.name: Zyad Khan
items.title: Associate Director - Procurement, Contracts and ESG
items.risk: Developing champion whose advocacy is real but not yet converted to a management-committee presentation or finished TCO; patience may erode if the conflict extends into H2 2026.

items.name: Farzan Almarzooqi
items.title: AVP Applications and Digitization IT Director
items.risk: IT integration feedback flagged on 30 Sep 2025 never completed; unaddressed Oracle EBS integration scoping could delay a restart.

items.name: Yamen Hmidan
items.title: Associate Director-Digital Transformation
items.risk: Digital-transformation evaluator engaged only at the 30 Sep 2025 demo; not re-engaged since.

items.name: Samyukta Rajendran
items.title: Data Analyst
items.risk: Operational contact who confirmed the Oracle EBS stack; useful for integration detail but no decision authority.

items.name: Ajaz Padder
items.title: PMO Manager
items.risk: PMO contact on record but not active in recent conversations.

champion_strength.summary: Zyad Khan is a developing champion: he rejected the in-house build in Zycus's favour, planned a 3-year TCO model, framed a modular start-small approach, and has a line to budget authority (reports to the SVP of IT, appeared jointly with Jason Tranter on 18 May 2026). Still missing: a management-committee presentation, upper-management escalation, and a finished TCO/business case. Engagement is active but paused by the spend freeze, not cooling.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole: Zyad Khan, Evaluator; Avoma 11 Feb 2026; SF Next_Step__c 30 Jul 2025 - 18 May 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin intake and Merlin insights were demoed on 30 Sep 2025 and Jason Tranter engaged positively on Zycus's AI maturity, but the deal has since narrowed to a light CLM + iSupplier repository with zero AI/Merlin mention across eight Next_Step entries from Jul 2025 to May 2026. Merlin is bundled in Products but is not part of the buyer's current buying conversation.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score is 3.0, AIS_Status Negative: eight dated Next_Step entries mention no AI, Merlin, agentic, or GenAI, and the 12 Feb 2026 scope contraction to 'CLM + iSupplier repository only' removed any AI positioning. The 30 Sep 2025 call did show early Merlin-intake curiosity from Jason and Zyad, so the appetite existed but has been overtaken by budget/timing constraints, confirming the field's read rather than contradicting it.

items.detail: Conflict-driven freeze on all non-essential spend has paused the project since 19 Mar 2026 with no reopen date.
Minimum-scope (CLM + iSupplier) price risks being uncompetitive per Zyad's own concern, threatening the unbudgeted approval.
Close date has slipped six times since Mar 2026 to 31 Dec 2026 with no committed restart milestone.
The true economic buyer (SVP of IT) is named only by role and never engaged; approval depends on a quarterly management committee.

confidence_signals.summary: Buyer engagement is warm but dormant on budget: constructive inbound through 18 May 2026, then quiet on their side as the freeze holds and the account changed owner on 29 Jun 2026.
items: Last genuine two-way buyer exchange 18 May 2026 (in-person office meeting arranged by Zyad Khan).
No buyer-committed dates for restart, RFP, or the upper-management decision.
Owner handover 29 Jun 2026 means the relationship must be re-warmed by Mohamad Alhakim.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We're the sole vendor DWTC wants for a light CLM + iSupplier repository, championed by Zyad Khan, but the whole deal is frozen by a conflict-driven spend freeze with no restart date, and the one thing between us and progress is getting budget reopened and the minimum-scope price approved.

win_position.text: Zycus is the only vendor in the race: Zyad Khan rejected the audit-suggested in-house build on 11 Feb 2026 ('I'll bring an off the shelf product') and no peer vendor has ever been named by the buyer.
Functional fit is strong and the champion is engaged: Zyad wants the CLM repository's renewal/expiry alerting (30 Sep 2025 demo) and is building an audit-driven business case with a 3-year TCO.

The deal is paused by force majeure, not progressing: all non-essential DWTC spend has been frozen since 19 Mar 2026 with the event calendar cancelled and no committed restart date (Next_Step__c 18 May 2026).

Price is a live threat even at sole-source: Zyad himself warned on 12 Feb 2026 that at the reduced scope 'min pricing will make us uncompetitive', and the real economic buyer (SVP of IT) has never been engaged.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is stalled: the close date has slipped six times since Mar 2026 to 31 Dec 2026 and no buyer-facing milestone has been executed since the 18 May 2026 meeting.
The account changed hands from Dan Quinn to RVP Mohamad Alhakim on 29 Jun 2026, so the primary relationship still needs to be re-established.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer stays constructively engaged: Zyad Khan arranged an in-person office meeting on 18 May 2026 and a margin-protection pitch 'seemed to resonate with VP' Jason Tranter, who agreed to raise it with upper management.
Commitment is verbal only: no RFP has opened, no budget is confirmed, and there is no signed scope, so DWTC has made no contractual step.

deal_risk.text: Do-nothing is the dominant risk: the conflict/blockade has indefinitely delayed the CLM decision, so the deal could quietly die of inertia rather than lose to a rival.
Scope has narrowed to CLM + iSupplier repository, a defensive cost-cutting signal that both shrinks the deal and pressures pricing below the minimum threshold.

deal_momentum.evidence: Paused since 19 Mar 2026; six close-date slips; no buyer milestone since 18 May 2026; owner just changed.

deal_risk.evidence: Budget freeze plus scope contraction plus minimum-price competitiveness worry.

scope_change.from: Source-to-Pay multi-module (iRisk + iContract + iSupplier/Merlin), original ~$110k opportunity
scope_change.detail: On 12 Feb 2026 Zyad Khan reduced scope 'back to CLM + iSupplier repository only at this phase (though interested in other modules later)', a defensive cost-cutting move under the budget freeze that both shrinks the deal and risks the minimum price being uncompetitive.

customer_preference.evidence: Zyad Khan chose off-the-shelf Zycus over an in-house/audit build on 11 Feb 2026 and praised the modular 'start small and scale up' fit, but tempered it with 'min pricing will make us uncompetitive' (12 Feb 2026).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative audit pressure ('mocking this contract management as obsolete') plus a DocuSign 5,000-envelope consolidation lever, but no quantified savings/ROI; Zyad still needs to build the 3-year TCO.

items.trigger: Ownership reassigned Dan Quinn -> Mohamad Alhakim; deal paused with no restart date
items.expected_effect: Preserves the primary relationship through the handover and surfaces when budget could reopen.

items.action: Send Zyad a quantified 3-year TCO that funds itself via DocuSign envelope consolidation.

items.expected_effect: Arms the champion's audit business case so the spend can clear an unbudgeted approval when the freeze lifts.

items.action: Reconnect Jason Tranter on the margin-protection case he agreed to raise with upper management.

items.expected_effect: Keeps the executive sponsorship warm and routes toward the SVP of IT economic buyer.

items.action: Re-engage Farzan Almarzooqi to close Oracle EBS integration scoping ahead of any restart.
items.trigger: Samyukta confirmed Oracle EBS on 13 Feb 2026; IT integration feedback promised 30 Sep 2025 never delivered
items.expected_effect: Removes an integration unknown so a restart can move straight to RFP without IT delay.

expansion_context.note: No prior Closed-Won deal on this account in the evidence; this is a new-business pursuit.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — On 12 Feb 2026 Zyad Khan reduced scope 'back to CLM + iSupplier repository only at this phase (though interested in other modules later)', a defensive cost-cutt (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).

commentary.win_position: Win 34 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); momentum 56 vs stage-expected 52. Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — On 12 Feb 2026 Zyad Khan reduced scope 'back to CLM + iSupplier repository only at this phase (though interested….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 56 (moving forward): commercial topics entering (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 20 is anchored on the stage win (34), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 56, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

Functional fit is strong and the champion is engaged; Zyad wants the CLM repository's renewal/expiry alerting (30 Sep 2025 demo) and is building an audit-driven business case with a 3-year TCO

The deal is paused by force majeure, not progressing; all non-essential DWTC spend has been frozen since 19 Mar 2026 with the event calendar cancelled and no committed restart date (Next_Step__c 18 May 2026)

Price is a live threat even at sole-source; Zyad himself warned on 12 Feb 2026 that at the reduced scope 'min pricing will make us uncompetitive', and the real economic buyer (SVP of IT) has never been engaged
